2606.28128.title: PhysisForcing: Physics Reinforced World Simulator for Robotic Manipulation
2606.28128.paper_summary: Video generation models have emerged as a promising paradigm for embodied world simulation. However, both general-domain video generators and robot-specific data fine-tuned models can still produce physically implausible manipulations, including discontinuous motion trajectories and inconsistent robot-object interactions, which limits their reliability as world simulators. Through extensive experiments, we find that such physical instability mainly arises from two factors: deformation of moving objects and implausible spatio-temporal correlations among interacting entities, particularly during contact. Building on this observation, we propose PhysisForcing, a scalable training framework that strengthens physical consistency by focusing supervision on physics-informative regions through joint optimization of pixel-level and semantic-level features. The framework consists of a pixel-level trajectory alignment loss, which supervises DiT features using reference point trajectories, and a semantic-level relational alignment loss, which aligns DiT features with inter-region relations extracted from a frozen video understanding encoder. Extensive experiments on R-Bench, PAI-Bench, and EZS-Bench show that PhysisForcing consistently improves embodied video generation over strong baselines, improving the Wan2.2-I2V-A14B and Cosmos3-Nano base models on R-Bench by 22.3\% and 9.2\% (7.1\% and 3.7\% over vanilla finetuning), with the Cosmos3-Nano variant attaining the best overall score. Beyond generation, as a world model under the WorldArena action-planner protocol it raises the closed-loop success rate from 16.0\% to 24.0\% and further improves downstream policy success, indicating that physically aligned video models yield stronger representations for robotic manipulation.
2606.28128.paper_summary_zh: 視頻生成模型已成為具身世界模擬的一個有前景的範式。<lb/>然而，通用領域的視頻生成器和針對機器人特定數據進行微調的模型仍然可能產生物理上不合理的操作，包括不連續的運動軌跡和不一致的機器人-物體互動，這限制了它們作為世界模擬器的可靠性。<lb/>通過廣泛的實驗，我們發現這種物理不穩定性主要源於兩個因素：移動物體的變形和互動實體之間不合理的時空關聯，特別是在接觸期間。<lb/>基於這一觀察，我們提出了PhysisForcing，一個可擴展的訓練框架，通過對物理信息區域的集中監督來加強物理一致性，通過像素級和語義級特徵的聯合優化。<lb/>該框架由一個像素級軌跡對齊損失組成，該損失使用參考點軌跡來監督DiT特徵，以及一個語義級關係對齊損失，該損失將DiT特徵與從凍結的視頻理解編碼器中提取的區域間關係對齊。<lb/>在R-Bench、PAI-Bench和EZS-Bench上的廣泛實驗表明，PhysisForcing在強基線上始終改善具身視頻生成，將R-Bench上的Wan2.2-I2V-A14B和Cosmos3-Nano基礎模型的性能分別提高了22.3\%和9.2\%（相較於普通微調的7.1\%和3.7\%），其中Cosmos3-Nano變體獲得了最佳的整體得分。<lb/>除了生成，作為WorldArena行動規劃者協議下的世界模型，它將閉環成功率從16.0\%提高到24.0\%，並進一步改善下游策略的成功率，這表明物理對齊的視頻模型為機器人操作提供了更強的表示。
2606.28128.author: Peiwen Zhang et.al.
2606.28128.authors: Peiwen Zhang, Yufan Deng, Shangkun Sun, Juncheng Ma, Duomin Wang, Jonas Du, Zilin Pan, Ye Huang, Hao Liang, Songyan Huang, Ruihua Zhang, Enze Xie, Ming-Yu Liu, Daquan Zhou
